title: Remove noop from squash help
body:
Signed-off-by: TomSweeneyRedHat <tsweeney@redhat.com>

Removes noop note from the squash on bud --help.  This will need
to be vendored into Podman to address https://github.com/containers/libpod/issues/2568

comments.body: I was going to vendor this into Podman when it got merged, but I think I'll hold off until @giuseppe #1395 gets in.

:pushpin: Commit 1edc893 has been approved by `@vrothberg`

<!-- @rh-atomic-bot r=@vrothberg 1edc8939f49b50a316c79c7c5bd0beab527d2fbc -->

@rhatdan what can be done with this?  It was all green but didn't merge after @vrothberg ok'd it.  I tried with him as the approver, now homu is failing?

It's always the "easy" ones that cause me the more CI grief....

@TomSweeneyRedHat when you lock on the "Details" link right of the test, you'll find the Travis interface where we can restart all or individual tests. Once they all passed, homu should be happy and the PR will be merged.

Thanks @vrothberg!  I've just gotten so use to the details link not being there when homu died that I didn't dive in there.  But even if I had I probably would not have seen the retest link, so thanks for the ptr!
IDK, what the heck is up with homu continuously failing.  I've rebased and repushed in hopes of getting past it.

:pushpin: Commit 699dd73 has been approved by `@rhatdan`

<!-- @rh-atomic-bot r=@rhatdan 699dd73f1f03ff33cd7ed75c56789f65e69446e0 -->
:zap: Test exempted: pull fully rebased and already tested.
